mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly signals expanding output capacity and improving economic fundamentals, which corporate earnings benefit from, driving S&P 500 prices higher over the next week.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and subsequent 5-day returns is ≤ 0.00 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it indicates deep labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If more than 50% of 20-day forward returns are positive when UNRATE is above 8%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4%) signals a collapse in consumer demand combined with tight monetary policy, which together produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy with weak demand
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 2%), increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect an accommodative monetary policy boosting residential investment, which lifts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases in the low-rate regime are associated with negative or flat 20-day returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms a healthy expansion in overall economic activity, which directly supports higher corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 prices upward over the upcoming quarter.
failure_condition: If the quarterly GDP growth rate and 60-day SPX returns show a negative rank correlation over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI level (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 310, it signals high and persistent inflation that forces the Fed to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment declines (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicate severe labor market distress that directly reduces consumer incomes and spending, generating a clear negative signal for S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month decline and UNRATE month-over-month increase
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 6%), a subsequent decline in the unemployment rate signals that the labor market is healing, which restores consumer confidence and spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If more than 50% of 60-day forward returns are negative when UNRATE declines from above 6%.
